zone_6b_notes: Missouri native perennial — extremely tough and drought tolerant in Zone 6b once established. Blooms June–September; flat-topped flower clusters in white, yellow, pink, and red. Spreads by rhizome and self-seeds; may become aggressive in rich soils. Best in lean, well-drained soil — rich soil causes floppy stems. Cut back after first flush of bloom to 3 inches for a second flush. Coronation Gold (yellow) and Paprika (red) are outstanding cultivars for Zone 6b. Excellent for cutting — fresh and dried. Attracts beneficial insects (lacewings, parasitic wasps, hoverflies).

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow in fall; transplant divisions spring or fall

water: drought tolerant — 0.5 inch/week; poor drainage kills plants
